text.title: LB High Availability Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example: SLA target 99.99% → topology selection → failure-domain analysis → final decision-record artefact.

section.title: Scenario
section.p: Service: payments-api. Customer base: US + EU. SLA target: 99.99%. Current state: one ALB in us-east-1 fronting 4 EC2 instances split across us-east-1a / 1b.

step.name: capture-sla
step.p: SLA = 99.99 → ≤ ~52 min/year allowable downtime. Customers in EU + US → multi-region required.

step.name: failure-domain-analysis

list.item: Process crash → ALB managed HA across AZs handles it. OK.
AZ-1a fails → 2 backends remain in 1b. OK if 1b can absorb load.
us-east-1 region fails → service is DOWN. SPOF.
Route 53 propagation delay → 5 min outage for some clients. SPOF if relying on DNS-based GSLB.

step.p: Two SPOFs at 99.99 tier. Redesign required.

step.name: pick-topology
step.p: Add us-west-2 region (mirror of us-east-1). Front both with AWS Global Accelerator (anycast). Endpoint group per region with `traffic_dial_percentage` 100 / 0 active-standby.

step.name: wire-zero-downtime
step.p: Add SIGTERM handler (see templates/sigterm-drain.py). Set ALB target group `deregistration_delay = 30`. Kubernetes `terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60`. Chaos test: kill one backend; observe 0 × 5xx.

step.name: record-and-validate
step.p: Validator passes (no SPOF, anycast at 99.99, drain wired). ADR is committed at `.aidocs/decisions/ha-payments.md`.
